data.title: Ares Capital cut to Market Perform at Raymond James on valuation
data.description:
Raymond James on Thursday downgraded Ares Capital (NASDAQ:ARCC) shares to Market Perform from Outperform in what analyst Robert Dodd called a "valuation driven rationale, not a credit/portfolio issue."

"We continue to see an attractive risk/reward with the high quality and highly diversified portfolio, very robust current credit quality, and a dividend that can be covered even if base rates decline materially," Dodd wrote in a note.

However, he believes such factors have already been priced in with the current share-price premium.

The business development company's net asset value per share has risen 4.9% from the start of the credit cycle in Q4 2021, the analyst noted, trouncing the BDC group median decline of 7.3%.

Seeking Alpha's Quant rating system gives ARCC a valuation grade of A, with the best mark in price-to-earnings and the worst score in EV-to-sales.

The RayJay downgrade comes a day after Ares (NASDAQ:ARCC) turned in Q4 earnings that missed the Wall Street consensus estimate, as net investment income and interest income from investments also came in lower than analysts expected.

The lower interest income was likely due to a "combination of greater than expected spread compression and timing of deployments," Dodd wrote.

Dodd's Market Perform rate compares with the Quant rating of Hold, as well as the average SA analyst rating and the average sell-side analyst rating, both at Buy.

ARCC shares edged down 1% in Tursday morning trading.

data.title: Symbotic falls as Raymond James downgrades stock on disappointing earnings
data.description:
Symbotic (NASDAQ:SYM) shares fell more than 17% in Thursday trade after Raymond James downgraded its rating from Outperform to Market Perform following a disappointing first quarter results.

The Massachusetts-based warehouse automation company reported both top-line and bottom-line misses on Wednesday, and guided second quarter figures below analysts’ estimates.

Symbotic (SYM) reported first quarter GAAP EPS of -$0.03, on a revenue of $486.69M. For the second quarter of fiscal 2025, Symbotic expects revenue of $510 million to $530 million, vs. $533.30M consensus.

Besides the earnings miss, analysts at Raymond James cited flat backlog and a slowing pace of system deployments as some of the reasons for the downgrade.

Analysts said that SYM’s success in reducing time-to-deployment was a core tenant of their thesis, and the current pace of 24 months shows “little improvement from the rate achieved two years ago”.

“With gross margins expected in the mid-twenties, the company needs to comfortably double its deployed system over the next few years to maintain rapid revenue growth, generate material EBITDA to support its trading multiple, and be able to have capacity to sign meaningful new clients,” the analysts added.

data.title: Kellanova tops estimates as Mars' buyout of the company draws closer
data.description:
Kellanova (NYSE:K) edged 0.2% higher in early trading on Thursday after posting a mixed performance with its Q4 earnings report. The company's GAAP net sales decreased 2% year-on-year due to adverse foreign currency translation. However, organic net sales, which exclude currency impact, increased by 7%, driven by growth in both volume and price/mix.

Regional performance varied, with North America experiencing a 2% decrease in both reported and organic net sales. Despite this, the region's reported operating profit increased by 28%, while adjusted operating profit grew by 8% (9% excluding currency translation). In Europe, reported net sales decreased by 2%, with organic net sales declining by 1%. However, the region's reported operating profit increased by 24%, and adjusted operating profit rose by 32% (33% excluding currency).

Reported operating profit saw a significant increase of 62% compared to a year ago. The growth was attributed to favorable mark-to-market impacts, improved gross profit margin due to productivity and moderating supply chain costs, and reduced overhead. On an adjusted basis, excluding mark-to-market and one-time charges, operating profit increased by 14%, and by 20% when excluding currency translation.

EPS was reported at $0.92 vs. $0.83 consensus and $0.78 a year ago.

"Led by our strong emerging markets presence, we sustained better-than-expected top-line growth amidst challenging industry conditions, and we improved our profit margins faster than we had anticipated. We also embarked on an exciting next phase, as we prepare to combine with Mars," stated CEO Steve Cahillane.

Last year, Mars agreed to acquire Kellanova for $83.50 per share in cash, totaling $35.9 billion including assumed net leverage. The acquisition marks one of the largest transactions in the food industry in recent years, combining two iconic businesses with complementary categories, markets, and portfolios of beloved brands. The merger unites Mars, a global leader in pet care, snacking, and food, with Kellanova, a company known for its global snacking, international cereal and noodles. The acquisition is expected to close in the first half of this year.

data.title: Medexus targets $100M annual revenue for GRAFAPEX within 5 years post-launch
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Medexus Pharmaceuticals (OTCQX:MEDXF) Q3 2025

Management View

  • CEO Ken d'Entremont highlighted the successful FDA approval of GRAFAPEX, the branded name for treosulfan for injection in the U.S., which holds Orphan Drug Designation, ensuring at least seven years of regulatory exclusivity. The company anticipates a commercial launch by April 2025 and projects annual product-level revenue for GRAFAPEX to exceed $100 million within five years after launch.
  • Fiscal Q3 2025 performance showed stable revenue of $30 million, an increase from $25.2 million in the prior year. Adjusted EBITDA grew to $5.8 million, up from $3.2 million last year, supported by financial discipline initiatives and preparation costs for the GRAFAPEX launch.
  • CFO Brendon Buschman noted, "This quarter, we are pleased to have generated $5.8 million of adjusted EBITDA from $30 million of revenue for an adjusted EBITDA margin over 19%."
  • The company also completed a CAD30 million public offering in January 2025, strengthening its balance sheet to support GRAFAPEX preparation and other growth initiatives.

Outlook

  • Management expects GRAFAPEX to be commercially available by April 2025. The FDA approval triggers a $15 million milestone payment to its partner Medac, payable in installments through January 2026, with options for deferral.
  • Rupall faces generic competition in Canada following the expiration of its market exclusivity in January 2025, and Medexus has initiated strategic measures to support the product in this new context.
  • The company aims to maintain stability in its base business while leveraging GRAFAPEX as a significant growth driver over the coming years.

Financial Results

  • Fiscal Q3 2025 revenue reached $30 million, up from $25.2 million in the prior year, driven by growth in Rupall net sales and IXINITY buying patterns. Gross profit was $15.2 million, compared to $12.7 million last year, with a gross margin of 50.7%.
  • Adjusted EBITDA grew to $5.8 million, an increase of $2.6 million year-over-year, reflecting financial discipline and customer buying trends. Net income for the quarter was $0.7 million, marking the fourth consecutive quarter of positive net income.
  • Cash on hand increased to $8.4 million as of December 31, 2024, up from $7 million at the end of the previous quarter, supported by operating cash flow of $6.7 million.

Q&A

  • Michael Freeman, Raymond James: Asked about IXINITY's strong performance. CEO d'Entremont clarified that the strength was due to higher-than-expected specialty pharmacy purchases, which may normalize in future quarters.
  • David Martin, Bloom Burton: Queried about GRAFAPEX pricing and Medicare reimbursement readiness. CEO d'Entremont noted ongoing reimbursement discussions and expects some payers to be in place by the product's April launch, though a full rollout will take several quarters.
  • Scott Henry, Alliance Global Partners: Highlighted the challenges with terbinafine hydrochloride. CEO d'Entremont confirmed the notice of deficiency from Health Canada as a significant challenge and stated Medexus will not invest further in the product.
  • Scott Henry also asked about the company’s financial readiness for milestone payments. CFO Buschman expressed confidence, stating, "With this equity raise, we've very adequately sort of de-risked our balance sheet."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed cautious optimism, focusing on the potential of GRAFAPEX while probing challenges in the broader product portfolio, including Rupall’s genericization and terbinafine delays. Sentiment was neutral to slightly positive.
  • Management maintained a confident tone, particularly regarding GRAFAPEX, emphasizing its revenue potential and operational preparedness. CEO d'Entremont stated, "Our core portfolio continues to provide Medexus with a solid foundation as we prepare for the next phase of our growth."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management exuded greater confidence, buoyed by the FDA approval and strengthened financial position.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Revenue increased from $26.3 million in Q2 2025 to $30 million in Q3 2025, driven by growth in Rupall and IXINITY buying patterns.
  • Adjusted EBITDA declined slightly from $6 million in Q2 2025 to $5.8 million in Q3 2025 due to higher SG&A expenses for GRAFAPEX preparations.
  • Management's tone was more optimistic in Q3, supported by GRAFAPEX's approval and the proceeds from the equity raise. Analysts’ questions shifted slightly from treosulfan approval timelines to commercialization strategies.

Risks and Concerns

  • Rupall’s market exclusivity expiration in Canada introduces the risk of revenue erosion. Management has initiated strategies to mitigate this impact.
  • Health Canada’s notice of deficiency regarding terbinafine hydrochloride poses significant challenges, leading to a halt in further investment in the product.
  • The company faces uncertainty in securing full reimbursement for GRAFAPEX in the U.S. at launch. Management acknowledged that it will take several quarters to align payers fully.
  • Gleolan’s modest growth and ongoing licensing discussions could result in Medexus exiting the product.

Final Takeaway

Medexus Pharmaceuticals delivered a strong fiscal Q3 2025 performance, marked by revenue growth and EBITDA improvement. The recent FDA approval of GRAFAPEX is a pivotal milestone, with management targeting annual revenue exceeding $100 million within five years. While challenges remain with Rupall and terbinafine hydrochloride, the company is strategically positioned to capitalize on GRAFAPEX’s U.S. launch and other pipeline opportunities. Investors should monitor reimbursement progress and competition in the Canadian market to assess the company’s ability to sustain growth.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: US Lawmakers to introduce bill to ban DeepSeek on government devices
data.description:
Two U.S. House lawmakers plan to introduce legislation Thursday to ban startup DeepSeek’s chatbot application from government-issued devices over security concerns the app could turn over user data to China’s government.

The bill, written by Rep. Darin LaHood (R-Ill.) and Josh Gottheimer (D-NJ), is similar to how Congress banned TikTok, another Chinese-controlled app, from government devices. ByteDance-owned (BDNCE) was eventually barred from operating in the U.S.

Gottenheimer said in a statement that it has been determined that DeepSeek’s code is “directly linked” to the Chinese Communist Party, and user data can be shared with state-owned China Mobile. He added that China Mobile, which has ties to the country’s military, has been banned in the U.S. by the Federal Trade Commission

“The Chinese Communist Party has made it abundantly clear that it will exploit any tool at its disposal to undermine our national security, spew harmful disinformation, and collect data on Americans,” Gottenheimer said in the statement. “Now, we have deeply disturbing evidence that they are using DeepSeek to steal the sensitive data of U.S. citizens. This is a five alarm national security fire.”

Neither LaHood nor DeepSeek immediately responded to Seeking Alpha’s requests for comment.

DeepSeek emerged last month when it released an AI model that reportedly outperformed Microsoft-backed (MSFT) OpenAI in many third-party tests and sparked debate about American dominance in the AI Sector.

data.title: Stocks open marginally higher as job cuts jump, initial jobless claims come in hotter
data.description:
U.S. stocks opened slightly higher on Thursday, while yields were little changed as job cuts jumped in January over the prior month and initial jobless claims came in hotter.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SPX) was +0.2% minutes after the opening bell, while the blue-chip Dow (DJI) was +0.1%. The Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was also +0.1%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was unchanged at 4.43%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) was up nearly 2 basis points to 4.21%.

Market sentiment took a hit after Qualcomm (QCOM) and Ford (F) both fell over 4% after their earnings.

Wall Street's major market averages closed higher Wednesday, overcoming earlier pressure brought on in part by a slump in shares of Magnificent Seven tech heavyweight Alphabet.

"After a severe allergic reaction on Monday after the tariff news, markets continued to be relatively sedated yesterday as investors continued to price out the chance of aggressive tariffs," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said.

In terms of more detail on what drove the moves yesterday, the ISM services print added fuel to the bond rally, as the prices paid component fell back to 60.4, after spiking to 64.4 the previous month, Reid added.

Labor reports take the spotlight in today's economic calendar.

The number of job cuts announced by U.S. employers jumped 28% to 49,795 in January from December, but it fell 40% from January 2024, according to Challenger Gray & Christmas data.

Initial jobless claims rose more than expected in the past week.

The Q4 productivity and costs report will also be released before the market opens, and the Fed balance sheet will come in later in the day.

"U.S. productivity and unit labor cost data is important but not reliable in real time. Automation and technology should increase productivity over time, as will social trends," UBS' Paul Donovan said.

Tech will once again be under the radar, with Amazon (AMZN) reporting its Q4 earnings after the bell.

data.title: Arm falls after royalty outlook misses estimates, analysts largely keep bullish views
data.description:
Shares of Arm Holdings (NASDAQ:ARM) fell about 7% on Thursday after fiscal fourth-quarter outlook came in line with estimates but royalties missed expectations, despite third-quarter results drawing praise from Wall Street analysts.

KeyBanc Capital Markets maintained its Overweight rating on Arm with a $195 price target.

Analysts led by John Vinh said Arm posted "strong" fiscal third-quarter results, which beat across royalties and licensing, and guided fiscal fourth quarter (March) in line. The analysts added that Royalty strength was driven by strong smartphone (Client) shipments, an initial ramp of compute subsystems, or CSS, in mobile (Mediatek D9400) and server CPU shipments (Graviton, Axion), while IoT showed signs of a recovery.

The analysts noted that fiscal fourth-quarter revenue guidance was in line, but royalties missed expectations as Armv9 penetration rate remains flat at 25% for three quarters, likely being impacted by Qualcomm's (QCOM) Snapdragon APs, which use a Nuvia (a Qualcomm subsidiary) CPU based on Armv8.

Adoption is still expected to reach 60% to 70% in the long term, but penetration growth could likely moderate until Qualcomm moves to Armv9, according to the analysts.

Jefferies kept its Buy rating and raised the price target on Arm's stock to $195 from $170.

Analysts Janardan Menon and his team said they forecast Arm's revenue growth to be 24% in fiscal year 2025 driven by licensing and royalty growth of 31% and 19%, respectively.

However, the analysts added that they expect the growth contributions to switch in fiscal 2026 & fiscal 2027, as royalties benefit from a rising v9 and CSS contribution, the recovery from the Internet of Things, or IoT, and networking weakness this year, and an increasing share of cloud compute.

Arm should benefit from the expected move to low-cost inferencing at the edge, according to Menon and his team.

Needham reiterated its Hold rating on Arm citing valuation, with no price target on the stock.

Analysts led by Charles Shi said Arm delivered a beat-and-raise quarter and raised its fiscal year 2025 (March) outlook. In the third quarter, both royalty and licensing revenues exceeded the estimates. For the March quarter, Arm expects a small decline in royalty revenues due to smartphone seasonality and a potential pullback in IoT revenues, offset by record licensing revenue supported by a couple of large renewals.

The analysts added that overall, Arm is overdelivering the fiscal year 2025 targets set at the time of the IPO. Beyond fiscal 2025, Arm expects to maintain 20% year-over-year growth in fiscal years 2026 and 2027, and believes that the company will transition from licensing-driven growth in fiscal 2024-25 to royalty-driven growth in fiscal 2026 and beyond.

data.title: Peloton shares race higher on improved FY25 outlook
data.description:
Peloton (NASDAQ:PTON) shares were racing higher ahead of Thursday’s opening bell as the company’s improved outlook for FY25 and narrower loss from a year ago helped offset declining memberships.

“We see significant opportunities ahead, but we have a steep hill to climb to reach sustained, profitable growth,” Peloton said in a letter to its shareholders.

“While it’s still early days for our new CEO, it's already clear where we need to focus to serve this purpose: innovation on new products and experiences that lead to even better outcomes for Members; presence in more places so we can meet Members wherever they are; more ways to connect Members with Peloton and our supportive community; and improved unit economics with a cost structure that’s right-sized for our business,” the letter said.

While Peloton (NASDAQ:PTON) attempts to achieve those objectives, it raised its adjusted EBITDA outlook for FY25 by $60M to a range of $300M to $350M from prior range of $240M to $290M, and its free cash flow target to at least $200M from prior guidance of $125M.

The results for the fiscal second quarter were mixed as the company continues to struggle to obtain more connected fitness subscribers. While the company exceeded its expectations, there were 4% fewer members in FQ2 from a year ago, and 4% fewer paid connected fitness subscriptions. This resulted in a 21% decline in connected fitness product revenue and 1% drop in subscription revenue, both of which contributed to a 9% drop in revenue to $673.9M, $20.6M above expectations. The net result was a net loss of $92M, or ($0.24) per share, 5 cents below estimates.

Looking specifically at holiday sales, which typically represents 40% of annual hardware sales, connected fitness products revenue exceeded expectations due to premium-priced hardware sales, namely Tread and Tread+. But the increased sales in these products resulted in inventory constraints that temporarily led to longer delivery times, delaying some Tread+ deliveries to Q3.

As a result of Peloton’s (NASDAQ:PTON) improved outlook for FY25, shares are up 18% in premarket trading.

data.title: Skyworks Solutions sinks as Apple plans to cut content demand for new iPhone
data.description:
Skyworks Solutions' (NASDAQ:SWKS) shares sank more than 25% during early Thursday trading after the company reported that its largest customer, Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL), plans to reduce its dependence on Skyworks for its iPhone 17 model.

Skyworks Chief Financial Officer Kris Sennesael detailed the development during the company's Q1 fiscal 2025 earnings call on Wednesday evening.

"Over the past 25 years, we have built a strong technology company, a leader in RF connectivity for mobile solutions, and expanded those RF capabilities with analog and mixed signal expertise, in our growing broad markets business," Sennesael said.

"As it relates to the upcoming phone cycle, expected to be launched in the fall of 2025, the Skyworks team developed a suite of high-performance RF solutions," he added. "Despite our rich product offering, we did not get the result that we targeted. Although we were able to secure multiple sockets, including several highly integrated RF modules, our content position is expected to be down 20% to 25%."

However, Sennesael said his company is already developing product solutions for the next generation, the iPhone 18, as well as pursuing opportunities with other mobile customers.

"We expect those opportunities to partially offset the revenue decline at the large customer in fiscal '26 and position us for growth in fiscal '27," he added.

The news prompted downgrades from multiple investment firms.

Stifel downgraded Skyworks to a Hold rating from a Buy, and reduced its price target by about 40% to $62 from $105.

"iPhones represent ~85% of Apple-related revenue for SWKS, so this dynamic is expected to significantly impact F4Q (we forecast <$900mn in revenue vs. ~$1.05bn consensus prior to the print) and FY26 (we forecast $3.6bn-$3.7bn range vs. ~$3.99bn prior consensus)," said Stifel analysts, led by Ruben Roy, in an investor note.

"We could see SWKS' story start to turn around in FY27 with the ramp of iPhone 18, contingent on progress with Apple's internally developed modem, on which SWKS has potential to gain content despite continued dual-sourcing of the dual receive module," Roy added.

B. Riley Securities also downgraded Skyworks to Neutral from Buy and slashed its price target to $65 from $110.

"Recall, Apple is 69% of annual revenue, with as management noted, the iPhone encompassing 85% of sales," said B. Riley analysts Craig Ellis and Stacy Che, in a Thursday investor note. "High-level analysis could indicate, at the midpoint, an annual revenue loss of ~$525M/13% beginning in F4Q25, which to us seems historic for SWKS. We further believe that while some capacity conversion to Android platforms is possible, it would likely recapture only a small minority of lost sales in the next 12-18 months, and since SWKS is vertically integrated, share loss is margin compressing."

Mizuho Securities downgraded the stock to Neutral from Outperform and reduced its price target by a similar margin to $62 from $105.

"For SWKS DecQ, we believe AAPL ~72% of revenue, and we believe a 20-25% RF content loss could be a challenge to current underutilization at ~65%," said Mizuho analysts, led by Vijay Rakesh, in an investor note. "Also, Android rev was flat q/q (~$75M) in DecQ, with Pixel (GOOG)(GOOGL)/Galaxy (OTCPK:SSNLF) muted as China handsets move to mid- and entry-level handsets, driving lower RF content and in-sourcing domestic RF."
